# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { Application, Permission, Request, Response } from "../deps.ts";

interface Env {
  REQUEST_URLS: string[];
}

async function main(request: Request, env: Env): Promise<Response> {
  if (!request.hasBody) {
    return new Response("Missing request body", { status: 400 });
  }

  const permissions = Deno.permissions.request({ name: "net" });

  const query = new URL(request.url).searchParams;

  const urls = query.getAll('url');

  let content = '';

  for (const url of urls) {
    try {
      const response = await fetch(url);
      if (!response.ok) {
        throw new Error(`HTTP error! status: ${response.status}`);
      }
      content += await response.text();
    } catch (error) {
      console.error(error);
      return new Response("Error fetching content", { status: 500 });
    }
  }

  return new Response(content, { headers: { "content-type": "text/plain" } });
}

export const get = async ({ request }: { request: Request }) => {
  try {
    await main(request, { REQUEST_URLS: Deno.args }));
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(error);
    return new Response("Internal Server Error", { status: 500 });
  }
};
